rulings.Case Title: HENRY CHAN ET AL VS. JUSTIN L. TIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Plaintiff'S Failure To Amend Following Sustaining Of Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT JUSTIN TIN, SIU CHAN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS FOR PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO AMEND FOLLOWING SUSTAINING OF DEMURRER Off Calendar. Duplicate. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE FULLER VS. FARMERS INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT ANDREA CHYNOWETH DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, in part, and OVERRULED, in part. The demurrers to cause of action 2 and 7 are overruled. D improperly asserts facts not in the Complaint. The demurrer to causes of action 4, 5, and 6 are sustained without leave to amend. Each of these causes of action are remedies, not standalone causes of action. To the extent Plaintiff wishes to re-allege these claims as different causes of action, Plaintiff may do so. The demurrer to cause of action 8 is sustained without leave to amend. Section 427.6 provides for a Temporary Restraining Order for harassing conduct following a petition, as opposed to a Complaint. Section 1708 does not provide a cause of action for harassment. To the extent Plaintiff wishes to re-allege these claims as different causes of action, Plaintiff may do so. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE FULLER VS. FARMERS INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT ANDREA CHYNOWETH MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. Paragraph 110 relies on paragraphs 48-52, which allege specific instances, on various dates spread across seven years, of deliberate conduct targeted at Plaintiff that are sufficient to pray for punitive damages. To the extent that particular allegations within those paragraphs are irrelevant or improper, the motion does not ask to strike those allegations. Additionally, Paragraph 111 is not improper or irrelevant because it is simply a summary of damages that are already alleged throughout the Complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
